entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R. Br., a crop adapted to low soil phosphorus in the Sahel

entities.name: LOW SOIL PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: Soil deficiency in phosphorus, a limiting factor for plant growth in the Sahel

entities.name: WEST AFRICAN SAHEL
entities.description: The geographical region where the study is focused

entities.name: AMMONIUM FERTILIZATION
entities.description: Application of NH4+-N to phosphorus fertilizer

entities.name: NITRATE FERTILIZATION
entities.description: Application of NO3--N to phosphorus fertilizer

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS FERTILIZATION
entities.description: Application of P fertilizer, specifically placed P

entities.name: MICRODOSE TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A method of applying small, precise amounts of fertilizer

entities.name: RHIZOTUBES
entities.description: Tubes used for nondestructive measurement of root length

entities.name: ROOT LENGTH
entities.description: The length of the plant roots, measured in topsoil and total

entities.name: SEEDLING HEIGHT
entities.description: The height of the pearl millet plant at the seedling stage

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The final amount of grain produced by the plant

entities.name: TIME TO FLOWERING
entities.description: The duration from sowing until the plant flowers

entities.name: TILLERING
entities.description: The production of side shoots or tillers

entities.name: VEGETATIVE DRY MATTER
entities.description: The dry weight of the vegetative parts of the plant at harvest

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which the plant uses water

entities.name: WATER USE
entities.description: The amount of water transpired by the plant during the cropping cycle

entities.name: LATERAL ROOT INITIATION
entities.description: The process of starting new lateral roots

entities.name: GRAIN FILLING
entities.description: The stage of crop development where seeds are filled with nutrients

entities.name: EARLY GROWTH STAGES
entities.description: The initial period of plant development after sowing

relationships.description: increases yield and promotes root growth
increases water use during grain filling
increases vegetative dry matter at harvest
crucial for survival in low P soil
region characterized by low P soil
